description: Implement Persistent Anti-Procrastination Reminder system using WorkManager and Foreground Service. Includes configurable reminder modes (Aggressive, Balanced, Minimal), customizable frequency, non-dismissible persistent notifications showing pending high-priority tasks, and motivational language. Also implement Settings screen for configuring reminder behavior, VIP contacts, and keyword management.
steps: Create ReminderService as a Foreground Service at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/service/ReminderService.kt that shows persistent non-dismissible notification with pending task summary and motivational text

Create ReminderWorker at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/service/ReminderWorker.kt using WorkManager for periodic reminders based on configured frequency (15min, 30min, hourly, morning+evening only)

Create ReminderScheduler at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/domain/engine/ReminderScheduler.kt that manages reminder scheduling based on mode: Aggressive (every 15 min with escalating language), Balanced (every 30 min), Minimal (morning + evening only)

Create MotivationalMessageProvider at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/domain/engine/MotivationalMessageProvider.kt with a bank of motivational and accountability-oriented messages that rotate based on time of day and task urgency

Create NotificationChannelManager at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/service/NotificationChannelManager.kt to set up notification channels for: task reminders (high importance, persistent), task suggestions (default importance), system notifications
Create BootReceiver at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/service/BootReceiver.kt that restarts the reminder service and re-schedules WorkManager jobs after device reboot
Register BootReceiver in AndroidManifest.xml with BOOT_COMPLETED intent filter
Create SettingsViewModel at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/presentation/settings/SettingsViewModel.kt managing all user preferences

Create SettingsScreen composable at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/presentation/settings/SettingsScreen.kt with sections: Reminder Settings (mode selector, frequency selector), Notification Settings (which apps to monitor), VIP Contacts (priority contact list), Keyword Management (add/remove action/urgency/time keywords), Export/Import, About

Create UserPreferencesRepository at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/data/repository/UserPreferencesRepository.kt using DataStore Preferences for storing: reminder mode, frequency, monitored apps, theme preference
Create VIP Contact management - add ContactPriority enum (VIP, HighPriority, Normal, Ignore) and UI for managing contact priorities in Settings

Create KeywordManagementScreen at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/presentation/settings/KeywordManagementScreen.kt for adding/removing/editing custom keywords in each category (action, urgency, time)

Create OnboardingScreen at app/src/main/java/com/procrastinationkiller/presentation/onboarding/OnboardingScreen.kt that guides user through: granting notification access permission, selecting apps to monitor, choosing reminder aggressiveness, optional biometric lock setup
Wire the onboarding flow to show on first launch and redirect to system settings for notification listener permission
Create unit tests for ReminderScheduler and MotivationalMessageProvider at app/src/test/java/com/procrastinationkiller/domain/engine/
Create unit tests for UserPreferencesRepository behavior
acceptance_criteria: Persistent notification shows current pending tasks with motivational message
Reminder frequency is configurable: 15min, 30min, hourly, morning+evening
Three reminder modes work: Aggressive, Balanced, Minimal
Boot receiver restarts reminder service after reboot
Settings screen allows configuring all reminder options
VIP contact system allows marking contacts with priority levels
Custom keywords can be added/removed through settings UI
Onboarding flow guides user through permission setup
WorkManager jobs are properly scheduled and survive app restart
Project compiles and unit tests pass
findings: All 101 tests pass (61 existing + 40 new). Build compiles cleanly. Added DataStore Preferences and Hilt WorkManager (hilt-work) dependencies. Used @ExperimentalFoundationApi OptIn for HorizontalPager in OnboardingScreen since Compose BOM 2024.02.00 still has pager as experimental. The existing ReminderMode enum (GENTLE, NORMAL, AGGRESSIVE, NUCLEAR) was kept as-is since it was defined in FEAT-001 and maps well to the feature requirements (Gentle=Minimal, Normal=Balanced, Aggressive=Aggressive). ContactPriority enum added separately from existing ContactEntity which already had isEscalationTarget field. ReminderWorker uses @HiltWorker with @AssistedInject for proper Hilt WorkManager integration.
